data.title: PCE Data Influences Market Trends With Rising Futures And Falling Bond Yields
data.description: According to Odaily, following the release of the PCE data, stock index futures rose while long-term bond yields declined. The initial reaction suggests that the milder inflation data might indicate a possibility for the Federal Reserve to further reduce interest rates. Robert Ruggirello, Chief Investment Officer at Brave Eagle Wealth Management, stated in a report that although additional rate cuts may still be several months away, this report supports the likelihood of one to two rate cuts by the Federal Reserve in 2025.

data.title: PCE data could help the Fed maintain one or two rate cuts this year
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Stock index futures moved higher after the PCE data was released, while long-term bond yields moved lower. The first reaction seemed to be that milder inflation data could mean that the Federal Reserve is likely to cut interest rates further. "While additional rate cuts may be several months away, we believe this report helps the Federal Reserve maintain one or two rate cuts in 2025," said Robert Ruggirello, chief investment officer of Brave Eagle Wealth Management, in a report. Futures contract traders continue to bet that the Federal Reserve will restart rate cuts in June as data showed that inflation rose in January in line with expectations. They still believe that the second rate cut in September is more likely than no rate cut. The data showed that the annual rate of the personal consumption expenditure price index fell from 2.6% in December to 2.5% last month, and the core PCE index fell to 2.6%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bloomberg analyst: BlackRock has reportedly added a Bitcoin ETF to a smaller model portfolio
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Eric Balchunas, senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, published an article on the X platform, saying that BlackRock has added the Bitcoin ETF IBIT to a smaller model portfolio. This is a small part of the allocation in the alternative category, but it is the first time that BlackRock has put the Bitcoin ETF into the investment model, so it is worth paying attention to, like testing the waters.

data.title: Bybit CEO: Hacker bounty platform LazarusBounty releases V1.1 version update
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Bybit CEO Ben Zhou announced that its hacker bounty platform LazarusBounty.com has released a V1.1 version update. The main updates include: adding a hacker address analysis function, supporting cross-chain asset viewing, and facilitating bounty hunters to track the flow of stolen funds; opening a Discord channel for bounty hunters to submit clues, and the system will automatically check duplicate addresses to avoid repeated submissions; the hacker address list can now be sorted by wallet balance; the verification report page adds the bounty hunter name display. In addition, Ben Zhou also said that since the platform went online, 2,167 reports have been received and processed, and the platform provides a 10% instant reward, with a total bounty pool of US$140 million.

data.title: In the past hour, the entire network has liquidated more than 30 million US dollars, mainly short orders
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that according to Coinglass data, the entire network had a liquidation of 30.8375 million US dollars in the past hour, of which short positions were liquidated by 26.2787 million US dollars and long positions were liquidated by 4.5588 million US dollars.

data.title: Orderly Integrates Berachain to Enhance Full-Chain Liquidity
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Web3 liquidity infrastructure protocol Orderly announced that it has completed integration with Berachain to enhance full-chain liquidity. Through this integration, Berachain ecosystem projects including DEX and perpetual contract protocols will be able to access Orderly's full-chain order book and instantly obtain Orderly's cross-chain liquidity. It is reported that Orderly is supported by more than 20 market makers including Wintermute and Riverside, and currently supports EVM and non-EVM chains including Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, Mantle and Solana.

data.title: The U.S. core PCE price index rose 0.3% in January, compared with expectations of 0.30% and the previous value of 0.20%.
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The U.S. core PCE price index monthly rate in January was 0.3%, expected to be 0.30%, and the previous value was 0.20%.
The U.S. core PCE price index annual rate in January was 2.6%, expected to be 2.60%, and the previous value was revised from 2.80% to 2.9%. (Jinshi)

data.title: The annual rate of core PCE in the United States was in line with expectations, hitting a new low since June 2024
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The U.S. core PCE price index in January recorded an annual rate of 2.6%, a new low since June 2024, in line with expectations.
The U.S. core PCE price index in January recorded a monthly rate of 0.3%, in line with expectations, a new high since October 2024. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. SEC Commissioner Releases "Crypto 2.0: Regulatory Spur": Coinbase Enforcement Actions Damage Regulatory Credibility
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Caroline A. Crenshaw, Commissioner of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, released "Crypto 2.0: Regulatory Whiplash", which pointed out that the enforcement action against Coinbase damaged the credibility of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's enforcement department, making people mistakenly believe that the law enforcement department will deploy enforcement resources based on election cycles or in a way that benefits those rich people, which has attracted criticism and sowed the seeds of distrust in the government. The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's responsibility is to do the right thing for investors, issuers, and capital markets.

data.title: Binance spokesperson: There is no massive selling of tokens, we are just helping users match transactions
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Social media had previously rumored that Binance was selling tokens during the market downturn, but Binance denied speculation about selling some of its assets and said it would not "dump" Solana and other tokens. A Binance spokesperson said: "Binance did not sell or sell tokens as claimed on social media. They misunderstood the role of Binance as an exchange. It only helps users match transactions. It is important not to jump to conclusions based on transaction screenshots." (Cointelegraph)
